title: Graphite image-filter intermediate format audit
summary: Direct Skia Graphite source evidence shows that image-filter intermediate devices are created through skif::Backend using a color type derived from the destination/layer color info or saveLayer flags, premultiplied alpha, and the filter context color space. Graphite does not provide one universal F16 image-filter intermediate policy or a standalone rgba16float-to-byte present quantization rule that Kanvas can apply to KAN-052.

graphiteSources.finding: Image filters ideally operate in the destination color type. Destinations with <=4 bytes per pixel and not RGBA_8888/BGRA_8888 are upgraded to kN32; otherwise the destination color type is kept. F16/F32-like destinations therefore remain high precision, but byte destinations generally remain byte/N32.
The image-filter backend is created with filterColorType and filterColorSpace derived from the destination/filter color info. The skif::Context then evaluates filters in that context.

SaveLayer uses kAlpha_8 for coverage layers, forces kRGBA_F16 when the kF16ColorType flag is set, otherwise uses image_filter_color_type(priorDevice->imageInfo().colorInfo()). Alpha is premul and color space is the layer override or prior device color space.

skif::Backend owns creation of offscreen intermediate renderable images and records surface props plus SkColorType. skif::Context carries the output device color space so intermediate surfaces match the final consumer and filtering is performed in destination color space.
FilterResult::AutoSurface creates intermediate devices via ctx.backend()->makeDevice(size, ctx.refColorSpace(), props), clears them transparent, then snaps a SkSpecialImage.

Graphite image-filter offscreen devices use SkImageInfo::Make(size, this->colorType(), kPremul_SkAlphaType, colorSpace) and Device::Make(..., LoadOp::kDiscard, "ImageFilterResult"). Graphite special images are produced through SkSpecialImages::MakeGraphite.

Renderable fallbacks preserve data type where possible: RGB_F16F16F16x and RGBA_F16Norm render through RGBA_F16; RGB_888x renders through RGBA_8888. The comment says Graphite intentionally avoids fallback that changes the underlying data type.
Graphite SpecialImage wraps a Graphite-backed SkImage and forwards image->imageInfo().colorInfo() to SkSpecialImage; non-Graphite images are converted with GetGraphiteBacked when possible.
RGBA_8888 maps to RGBA8/BGRA8, RGBA_F16 and RGBA_F16Norm map to RGBA16F. Floating-point formats do not auto-clamp; unsigned normalized formats do.
Graphite rejects unknown/unpremul render targets, requires color-type/format compatibility, and configures render passes from the target texture format plus write swizzle for the color type.
GPU-to-CPU readback builds color-space transform steps from source and destination color info, then creates a TextureFormatXferFn for the source texture format and destination CPU color type.
Readback conversion loads from the texture-format base color type, applies swizzles/color-space steps, and stores into the requested destination color type through SkRasterPipeline ops.

kanvasCurrentPolicy.intermediateFormat: GPUTextureFormat.RGBA16Float by default, constructor-scoped RGBA8Unorm fallback only
kanvasCurrentPolicy.intermediateEncoding: premul sRGB-coded values, not a true linear working space
kanvasCurrentPolicy.presentBoundary: textureLoad from intermediate_texture followed by final RGBA8Unorm output quantization
kanvasCurrentPolicy.imageFilterScratch: crop and blur image-filter scratch textures use SkWebGpuDevice.intermediateFormat

cpuReferencePolicy.storePolicy: float premul storage avoids 8-bit quantization drift until final read/PNG comparison

kan052Impact.reason: Graphite chooses image-filter intermediate color type from destination/layer color info rather than exposing a standalone F16-to-byte quantization policy; existing FOR-259/FOR-261/FOR-264/FOR-306 evidence remains diagnostic-only.

kanvasPolicyDecision.description: Do not change Kanvas intermediate format or present quantization from Graphite evidence alone. Keep KAN-052 blocked until a Kanvas-native policy proof provides reference, CPU/GPU, diff/stat, route diagnostics, fallback stability, and perf/memory impact.

nonClaims: No image-filter support expansion is claimed.
No renderer, shader, threshold, golden, or fallback policy changed.
No Ganesh or Graphite implementation is ported.
No SkSL compiler, IR, or VM is introduced.
No expected-unsupported or implementation-gap row is promoted to support.

nextDecisionOptions.option: Run a Kanvas-native policy proof ticket
nextDecisionOptions.description: Build a row-local proof matrix for intermediateFormat/present quantization candidates across KAN-052 residual, exact controls, precision-sensitive controls, route diagnostics, fallback stability, and perf/memory impact.
Maintain RGBA16Float intermediate plus RGBA8Unorm present output and keep KAN-052 blocked until complete local proof exists.
